completion_summary: Clarified chronology for the four gap laws: PL-010-065 is source-history only, PL-011-070 is a current transfer enactment with no self-sunset, PL-011-071 remains current and integrated, and PL-011-072 is later repealed but its section 133 text survives through the independent reenactment noted in the chronology report.

laws.title: Public Law 9-62 | Repealing the District of Columbia Home Rule Act
laws.status_basis: This repeal-history measure remains a current note-only law with no self-sunset.

current_implementation.detail: The existing note still needs to be trimmed to a concise repeal-history statement.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the repeal as a concise history note and remove any dump-style material.

laws.title: Public Law 9-63 | The Federal District Employment Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: The source remains unrecoverable, so no operative codification conclusion can be fixed from the available materials.

current_implementation.detail: A default Title 5 placement was chosen despite no recoverable statutory text.

laws.recommended_action: Retain only a concise source-limited historical note and do not invent operative text.

laws.title: Public Law 10-65 | Federal Judicial Reform Act ii
laws.status_basis: The recovered source is a judicial opinion, not enactment text, and it invalidates the challenged provisions.

current_implementation.detail: A judicial opinion was treated like a statutory note and parked in an unrelated title.

laws.recommended_action: Remove the dump and Trello link and keep only a short historical cross-reference note, if any.

laws.title: Public Law 10-66 | The Americans First Act
laws.status_basis: The act is an operative repealer with immediate effect and does not create a new permanent Code section.

current_implementation.detail: The note is still dump-heavy and anchored in an awkward subject area.

laws.recommended_action: Keep only concise historical notes recording the repeals.

laws.title: Public Law 10-67 | Ambassadorial Readjustment Act
laws.status_basis: The act creates permanent ambassadorial rules and needs real Title 22 codification.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is only a boilerplate summary with a dump and link.

laws.recommended_action: Codify the substantive Section 2 rules as a new Title 22 provision near 22 U.S.C. 2651a.

laws.title: Public Law 10-68 | American Jobs Act
laws.status_basis: The law is an operative repealer with immediate effect, so a concise historical note is sufficient.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is parked in the wrong subject area and still carries a full-text dump.

laws.recommended_action: Record the repeal of Public Law 3-20 as a concise historical cross-reference.

laws.title: Public Law 11-69 | DC Criminal Code Act
laws.status_basis: The source creates enforceable D.C. criminal and traffic rules, but it belongs outside permanent federal U.S. Code text.

current_implementation.detail: The current note treats a local D.C. criminal code as if it were federal permanent text.

laws.recommended_action: Exclude this local/District criminal code from permanent U.S. Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 11-70 | Supreme Court Please Act
laws.status_basis: The source directs a substantive transfer under Title 40 and should be moved to the proper anchor.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is anchored in the wrong title and only captures a boilerplate summary.

laws.recommended_action: Move the transfer to the 40 U.S.C. 6121 anchor and codify the duty transfer there.

laws.title: Public Law 11-71 | Supreme Court Needs Act
laws.status_basis: The act directly rewrites 28 U.S.C. section 1, so a note-alone treatment is insufficient.

current_implementation.detail: The note is parked at the right anchor but remains boilerplate.

laws.recommended_action: Actually amend 28 U.S.C. 1 to the new Chief Justice plus four associate justices text.

laws.title: Public Law 11-72 | No More Mariah Act
laws.status_basis: The act directly replaces Title 28 text and also records a repeal, so note-only treatment is not sufficient.

current_implementation.detail: The note is anchored at section 1, but the real target is 28 U.S.C. 133.

laws.recommended_action: Amend 28 U.S.C. 133 with the new District of Columbia judgeship text and record the repeal of Public Law 10-65 as repeal history.
